mnemonics: QUANTUM AI: Quantifying Uncertainty And Numbers To Understand Machines - A.I. Insight
keyPoints: Quantifying uncertainty is a core principle in AI, often using probability to model real-world ambiguity.
Measuring performance (e.g., accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score) quantifies how well a model performs its task.
Quantifying data (feature scaling, normalization) is a critical pre-processing step to improve model training and results.
commonMistakes: Confusing precision (how many selected items are relevant) with recall (how many relevant items are selected).
Applying quantification metrics meant for regression (like RMSE) to classification problems, and vice-versa.
Forgetting to quantify and account for uncertainty in a model's predictions, leading to overconfidence in incorrect outputs.
